title: Hybrid smartwatch
abstract: A hybrid smartwatch includes a digital display comprising an opening that extends from a bottom portion of the digital display to a top portion of the digital display, and one or more mechanical hands. Each mechanical hand is individually controlled by a motor that is positioned below the digital display, and each mechanical hand extends through the opening and is positioned between the digital display and a touch- sensitive screen. Each mechanical hand is rotatable about the digital display. The hybrid smartwatch includes a microcontroller and a computer-readable storage medium having one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to not cause information to be displayed on a portion of the digital display where the opening is located.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What Is Claimed Is: 1. A hybrid smartwatch, comprising: a digital display comprising an opening that extends from a bottom portion of the digital display to a top portion of the digital display; one or more mechanical hands, wherein each mechanical hand is individually controlled by a motor that is positioned below the digital display, wherein each mechanical hand extends through the opening and is positioned between the digital display and a touch- sensitive screen, wherein each mechanical hand is rotatable about the digital display; a microcontroller; and a computer-readable storage medium comprising one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to not cause information to be displayed on a portion of the digital display where the opening is located. 2. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 1, wherein the computer-readable storage medium further comprises one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to: receive one or more instructions to divide the digital display of the hybrid smartwatch into a plurality of portions; identify a current position of one or more mechanical hands of the hybrid smartwatch; determine a temporary position for each of the one or more mechanical hands in order to achieve the plurality of portions; 

 cause each of the one or more mechanical hands to be positioned in its corresponding temporary position, and cause information to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display. 3. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 2, wherein the one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to receive one or more instructions to divide the digital display of the hybrid smartwatch into the plurality of portions comprise one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to receive an instruction to display one or more of the following: a text message; an email message; or an alert. 4. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 2, wherein the one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to determine a temporary position for each of the one or more mechanical hands comprise one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to: determine whether moving the one or more mechanical hands from the current position to a 3/45 position involves fewer rotations than moving the one or more mechanical hands from the current position to a 9/15 position; and 

 in response to determining that moving the one or more mechanical hands from the current position to the 3/45 position involves fewer rotations, identify the 3/45 position as the temporary position, otherwise, identify the 9/15 position as the temporary position. 5. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 2, wherein the one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to cause each of the one or more mechanical hands to be positioned in its corresponding temporary position comprise one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to: instruct a first motor that controls movement of a first mechanical hand to move the first mechanical hand to its corresponding temporary position; and instruct a second motor that controls movement of a second mechanical hand to move the second mechanical hand to its corresponding temporary position. 6. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 2, wherein the one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to cause information to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display comprise one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to cause one or more of the following to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display: a text message; an email message; or an alert. 7. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 2, wherein the one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to cause information to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display comprise one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to not display information on the digital display where an opening is located, wherein the one or more mechanical hands extend through the opening. 8. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 2, wherein the computer-readable storage medium further comprises one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to move the one or more mechanical hands to a position that indicates a current time. 9. The hybrid smartwatch of claim 1, wherein the computer-readable storage medium further comprises one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the microcontroller to cause a Quick Response (QR) code to be displayed via the digital display, wherein the QR code is unique to the hybrid smartwatch and facilitates pairing with an electronic device. 10. A method of moving one or more mechanical hands of a hybrid smartwatch, the method comprising: receiving, by a microcontroller of a hybrid smartwatch, one or more instructions to divide a digital display of the hybrid smartwatch into a plurality of portions; identifying a current position of one or more mechanical hands of the hybrid smartwatch; determining a temporary position for each of the one or more mechanical hands in order to achieve the plurality of portions; causing each of the one or more mechanical hands to be positioned in its corresponding temporary position; and causing information to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein receiving one or more instructions to divide the digital display of the hybrid smartwatch into the plurality of portions comprises receiving an instruction to display one or more of the following: a text message; an email message; or an alert. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein determining a temporary position for each of the one or more mechanical hands comprises: 

 determining whether moving the one or more mechanical hands from the current position to a 3/45 position involves fewer rotations than moving the one or more mechanical hands from the current position to a 9/15 position; and in response to determining that moving the one or more mechanical hands from the current position to the 3/45 position involves fewer rotations, identifying the 3/45 position as the temporary position, otherwise, identifying the 9/15 position as the temporary position. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein causing each of the one or more mechanical hands to be positioned in its corresponding temporary position comprises: instructing a first motor that controls movement of a first mechanical hand to move the first mechanical hand to its corresponding temporary position; and instructing a second motor that controls movement of a second mechanical hand to move the second mechanical hand to its corresponding temporary position. 14. The method of claim 10, wherein causing information to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display comprises causing one or more of the following to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display: a text message; an email message; or an alert. 15. The method of claim 10, wherein causing information to be displayed in one or more of the plurality of portions of the digital display comprises not displaying information on the digital 

 display where an opening is located, wherein the one or more mechanical hands extend through the opening. 16. The method of claim 10, further comprising moving the one or more mechanical hands to a position that indicates a current time. 17. The method of claim 10, further comprising displaying a Quick Response (QR) code to via the digital display, wherein the QR code is unique to the hybrid smartwatch and facilitates pairing with an electronic device. 18. A method of moving one or more mechanical hands of a hybrid smartwatch, the method comprising: receiving, by an electronic device in communication with a hybrid smartwatch, an indication that the hybrid smartwatch is to be calibrated; capturing, by an image capture device in communication with the electronic device, an image of at least a portion of a digital display of the hybrid smartwatch, wherein the at least a portion of the digital display comprises one or more mechanical hands; performing image processing on the image to identify a current position associated with each of the one or more mechanical hands; determining, by the electronic device, a current actual time; and sending one or more instructions to the hybrid smartwatch, wherein the one or more instructions comprise instructions to move the one or more mechanical hands such that the hybrid smartwatch displays the current actual time. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising, in response to receiving the indication that the hybrid smartwatch is to be calibrated, sending the hybrid smartwatch an instruction to enter calibration mode, wherein the hybrid smartwatch is configured to display a position reference element via the digital display while in calibration mode. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein performing image processing on the image to identify the current position associated with each of the one or more mechanical hands comprises: determining a position of an end portion of each of the one or more mechanical hands; and determining a distance between each of the end portions and the position reference element. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein sending one or more instructions to the hybrid smartwatch comprises, for each of the one or more mechanical hands, determining a distance between the current position of the mechanical hand a position of the mechanical hand corresponding to the actual current time. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein sending one or more instructions to the hybrid smartwatch comprises sending one or more instructions to move the one or more mechanical hands the determined distance such that the hybrid smartwatch displays the current time.
